---
title: "Examples"
toc: false
---

Every example exists in both a Python version (`hecfdapy`, a Jupyter notebook you can download
and run) and an R version (`hecfdar`, a Quarto document). Each one reproduces a value validated
against the real HEC-FDA C#, and states that value alongside the result it computes.

- **00 -- Getting started**: install both packages and reproduce the deterministic 150000 EAD
  oracle from the quick start above.
- **01 -- Distributions**: density, CDF, quantile, and seeded sampling across the 13 ported
  distribution families.
- **02 -- Stage-damage curves**: build a structure inventory and compute a stage-damage curve,
  checked against the tractable Residential/Commercial oracle.
- **03 -- EAD simulation**: run the seeded Monte Carlo expected annual damage simulation across
  impact areas and reproduce the exact-100-iteration benchmark.
- **04 -- Alternatives capstone**: chain a scenario through EqAD annualization and
  with/without-project benefits, reproducing the full AlternativeComparisonReport pipeline.
